2026-04-28

library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(corrplot)

library(car)

Data

data <- read_excel("C:\\Users\\ACER\\OneDrive\\Dokumen\\AnregData.xlsx", sheet="2024")
head(data)
## # A tibble: 6 × 8
##   Provinsi      `Luas Kebakaran Hutan` `Kendaraan Bermotor` `Kepadatan Penduduk`
##   <chr>                          <dbl>                <dbl>                <dbl>
## 1 Aceh                           7257.              2770328                   98
## 2 Sumatera Uta…                  7032.              8137094                  215
## 3 Sumatera Bar…                  3052.              3083650                  139
## 4 Riau                          11028.              4766156                   75
## 5 Jambi                          5637.              2847874                   76
## 6 Sumatera Sel…                 15422.              4410213                  102
## # ℹ 4 more variables: `Curah Hujan` <dbl>, Suhu <dbl>,
## #   `Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi` <dbl>, `PM 2.5` <dbl>
str(data)
## tibble [34 × 8] (S3: tbl_df/tbl/data.frame)
##  $ Provinsi                    : chr [1:34] "Aceh" "Sumatera Utara" "Sumatera Barat" "Riau" ...
##  $ Luas Kebakaran Hutan        : num [1:34] 7257 7032 3052 11028 5637 ...
##  $ Kendaraan Bermotor          : num [1:34] 2770328 8137094 3083650 4766156 2847874 ...
##  $ Kepadatan Penduduk          : num [1:34] 98 215 139 75 76 102 105 281 92 264 ...
##  $ Curah Hujan                 : num [1:34] 2424 3185 4275 3289 2797 ...
##  $ Suhu                        : num [1:34] 24.8 25.7 25.4 27 26.5 ...
##  $ Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi: num [1:34] 7391 8245 5803 6882 2786 ...
##  $ PM 2.5                      : num [1:34] 41.2 99.2 11.6 80 75.5 ...

Statistika Deskpritif

summary(data)
##    Provinsi         Luas Kebakaran Hutan Kendaraan Bermotor Kepadatan Penduduk
##  Length:34          Min.   :     0.51    Min.   :  305187   Min.   :   10.0   
##  Class :character   1st Qu.:   680.52    1st Qu.: 1206667   1st Qu.:   57.0   
##  Mode  :character   Median :  3624.84    Median : 2809101   Median :  103.5   
##                     Mean   : 10265.56    Mean   : 4896056   Mean   :  755.9   
##                     3rd Qu.: 11351.91    3rd Qu.: 4677170   3rd Qu.:  276.8   
##                     Max.   :113089.51    Max.   :27104924   Max.   :16165.0   
##   Curah Hujan        Suhu       Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi     PM 2.5      
##  Min.   :1377   Min.   :22.77   Min.   :  653                Min.   :  3.19  
##  1st Qu.:2270   1st Qu.:25.43   1st Qu.: 1675                1st Qu.: 12.71  
##  Median :2725   Median :26.60   Median : 3764                Median : 24.07  
##  Mean   :2739   Mean   :26.29   Mean   : 5371                Mean   : 38.47  
##  3rd Qu.:3169   3rd Qu.:27.29   3rd Qu.: 6952                3rd Qu.: 68.25  
##  Max.   :4275   Max.   :28.76   Max.   :23319                Max.   :111.62
data.frame(
  Mean = sapply(data[, -1], mean), 
  Median = sapply(data[, -1], median), 
  SD = sapply(data[, -1], sd), 
  Min = sapply(data[, -1], min), 
  Max = sapply(data[, -1], max) 
)
##                                      Mean      Median           SD       Min
## Luas Kebakaran Hutan         1.026555e+04    3624.845 2.058172e+04      0.51
## Kendaraan Bermotor           4.896056e+06 2809101.000 6.860048e+06 305187.00
## Kepadatan Penduduk           7.558529e+02     103.500 2.752380e+03     10.00
## Curah Hujan                  2.739427e+03    2725.145 6.425736e+02   1377.22
## Suhu                         2.628824e+01      26.600 1.503135e+00     22.77
## Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi 5.371118e+03    3763.500 5.008394e+03    653.00
## PM 2.5                       3.847294e+01      24.070 3.196504e+01      3.19
##                                      Max
## Luas Kebakaran Hutan           113089.51
## Kendaraan Bermotor           27104924.00
## Kepadatan Penduduk              16165.00
## Curah Hujan                      4275.23
## Suhu                               28.76
## Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi    23319.00
## PM 2.5                            111.62

Histogram

library(reshape2) 
library(ggplot2)
data_long <- melt(data[, -1])
## No id variables; using all as measure variables
ggplot(data_long, aes(x = value, fill = variable)) +
  geom_histogram(color = "black", bins = 10, alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free") +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
  labs(
    title = "Histogram Seluruh Variabel",
    x = "Nilai",
    y = "Frekuensi"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 14) 
  )

Boxplot

library(reshape2) 
library(ggplot2)
data_long <- melt(data[, -1])
## No id variables; using all as measure variables
ggplot(data_long, aes(x = variable, y = value, fill = variable)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +
  labs(
    title = "Boxplot Seluruh Variabel",
    x = "Variabel",
    y = "Nilai"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )

Scatter Plot

library(reshape2) 
library(ggplot2)
data_long <- melt(data[, -1], id.vars = "PM 2.5")
ggplot(data_long, aes(x = value, y = `PM 2.5`)) +
  geom_point(color = "#3949AB", alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", color = "#E53935", se = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales = "free") +
  labs(title = "Hubungan Variabel Prediktor dengan PM 2.5",
       x = "Variabel Prediktor",
       y = "PM 2.5") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Matriks Korelasi

cor_matrix <- cor(data[, -1])  # Gapake variabel Provinsi
cor_matrix
##                              Luas Kebakaran Hutan Kendaraan Bermotor
## Luas Kebakaran Hutan                   1.00000000        -0.04220888
## Kendaraan Bermotor                    -0.04220888         1.00000000
## Kepadatan Penduduk                    -0.10328457         0.28919162
## Curah Hujan                           -0.38228133        -0.07697389
## Suhu                                   0.19444147         0.11106245
## Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi           0.10930516         0.87188603
## PM 2.5                                -0.12735051         0.27410433
##                              Kepadatan Penduduk Curah Hujan        Suhu
## Luas Kebakaran Hutan                 -0.1032846 -0.38228133  0.19444147
## Kendaraan Bermotor                    0.2891916 -0.07697389  0.11106245
## Kepadatan Penduduk                    1.0000000 -0.11775536  0.22877728
## Curah Hujan                          -0.1177554  1.00000000 -0.51770827
## Suhu                                  0.2287773 -0.51770827  1.00000000
## Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi          0.3580468 -0.01532780  0.05213266
## PM 2.5                                0.4137271  0.27368091 -0.13759645
##                              Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi     PM 2.5
## Luas Kebakaran Hutan                           0.10930516 -0.1273505
## Kendaraan Bermotor                             0.87188603  0.2741043
## Kepadatan Penduduk                             0.35804676  0.4137271
## Curah Hujan                                   -0.01532780  0.2736809
## Suhu                                           0.05213266 -0.1375965
## Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi                   1.00000000  0.3683580
## PM 2.5                                         0.36835800  1.0000000
corrplot(cor_matrix,
         method = "color",
         type = "upper",
         addCoef.col = "black",
         tl.col = "black",
         col = colorRampPalette(c("#D32F2F", "white", "#1976D2"))(200),
         number.cex = 0.7)

Model

model <- lm(`PM 2.5` ~ `Luas Kebakaran Hutan` + `Kendaraan Bermotor` + 
            `Kepadatan Penduduk` + `Curah Hujan` + Suhu + `Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi`, data=data)
summary(model)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = `PM 2.5` ~ `Luas Kebakaran Hutan` + `Kendaraan Bermotor` + 
##     `Kepadatan Penduduk` + `Curah Hujan` + Suhu + `Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi`, 
##     data = data)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -47.952 -19.858  -4.131   8.480  51.477 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                  Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## (Intercept)                     4.173e+01  1.198e+02   0.348   0.7304  
## `Luas Kebakaran Hutan`         -4.716e-06  2.958e-04  -0.016   0.9874  
## `Kendaraan Bermotor`           -3.035e-07  1.648e-06  -0.184   0.8553  
## `Kepadatan Penduduk`            4.390e-03  2.087e-03   2.103   0.0449 *
## `Curah Hujan`                   1.336e-02  9.964e-03   1.341   0.1912  
## Suhu                           -1.974e+00  4.008e+00  -0.492   0.6264  
## `Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi`  1.909e-03  2.351e-03   0.812   0.4240  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 28.81 on 27 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.3354, Adjusted R-squared:  0.1877 
## F-statistic: 2.271 on 6 and 27 DF,  p-value: 0.06657

Uji Kenormalan Residual

shapiro.test(residuals(model))
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  residuals(model)
## W = 0.93734, p-value = 0.05141
qqnorm(residuals(model))
qqline(residuals(model), col = "red")

Homoskedastisitas

library(lmtest)
bptest(model)
## 
##  studentized Breusch-Pagan test
## 
## data:  model
## BP = 7.9379, df = 6, p-value = 0.2427

Independensi Residual

dwtest(model)
## 
##  Durbin-Watson test
## 
## data:  model
## DW = 1.7123, p-value = 0.1371
## alternative hypothesis: true autocorrelation is greater than 0

Multikolinearitas

library(car)
vif(model)
##         `Luas Kebakaran Hutan`           `Kendaraan Bermotor` 
##                       1.473165                       5.080747 
##           `Kepadatan Penduduk`                  `Curah Hujan` 
##                       1.312238                       1.629762 
##                           Suhu `Jumlah Perusahaan Konstruksi` 
##                       1.443042                       5.514538

Outlier

rstudent(model)
##            1            2            3            4            5            6 
##  0.088134575  1.931905391 -2.001177306  1.327421726  1.605569953  1.889955857 
##            7            8            9           10           11           12 
##  1.102257319 -0.021114960 -0.043210856 -0.547301705 -0.002109144 -1.963270091 
##           13           14           15           16           17           18 
##  1.643081225  1.595426041 -1.465829395 -0.846289910 -0.222967005  0.142012153 
##           19           20           21           22           23           24 
## -0.571430438 -0.147475353 -0.019642451  0.984036295 -0.164708459  0.174427187 
##           25           26           27           28           29           30 
## -0.180176281 -0.831021442  0.390653936 -0.765855156 -0.199354463 -1.453771562 
##           31           32           33           34 
## -0.493657488 -0.496264413 -0.934240846 -0.986136098
plot(model, which = 1)

Leverage

hatvalues(model)
##          1          2          3          4          5          6          7 
## 0.20149577 0.05801590 0.23123350 0.10536084 0.04264398 0.04107569 0.11863532 
##          8          9         10         11         12         13         14 
## 0.05798420 0.13261571 0.16487074 0.99278259 0.67879203 0.23637043 0.21990178 
##         15         16         17         18         19         20         21 
## 0.54562280 0.07736894 0.15884276 0.20375881 0.81421677 0.29880491 0.12606176 
##         22         23         24         25         26         27         28 
## 0.04659143 0.07923155 0.13061025 0.12975234 0.06977617 0.27812387 0.05540995 
##         29         30         31         32         33         34 
## 0.09767962 0.26202954 0.07124479 0.12722973 0.06697824 0.07888731
plot(model, which = 5)
## Warning in sqrt(crit * p * (1 - hh)/hh): NaNs produced
## Warning in sqrt(crit * p * (1 - hh)/hh): NaNs produced

n <- nrow(data)
2 * (5 + 1) / n
## [1] 0.3529412

Cook’s Distance

cooks.distance(model)
##            1            2            3            4            5            6 
## 2.906993e-04 2.982037e-02 1.548473e-01 2.883124e-02 1.549811e-02 1.995675e-02 
##            7            8            9           10           11           12 
## 2.317833e-02 4.071148e-06 4.234775e-05 8.672835e-03 9.077732e-05 1.052368e+00 
##           13           14           15           16           17           18 
## 1.123095e-01 9.695331e-02 3.535500e-01 8.670973e-03 1.390062e-03 7.650299e-04 
##           19           20           21           22           23           24 
## 2.096681e-01 1.373779e-03 8.256195e-06 6.768009e-03 3.459545e-04 6.772914e-04 
##           25           26           27           28           29           30 
## 7.171632e-04 7.486030e-03 8.671828e-03 4.991622e-03 6.372710e-04 1.029568e-01 
##           31           32           33           34 
## 2.747540e-03 5.276099e-03 8.993161e-03 1.191004e-02
plot(model, which = 4)
